name: Corncob Design Language
description: Lightweight, accessible vanilla JS design system. Perfect for AI-assisted website and application development.
aiIntegrationGuide: See DESIGN_SYSTEM_FOR_AI.md for documentation on how AI systems should consume these files
layoutManifest: See layouts.json for default page and section layout rules.

codeGenerationWorkflow.rule: Use canonical component code before enhancing.
steps: Find the canonical component example in components.json or the component docs page.
Emit the documented markup without changing required structure, child order, or required classes.
Place the component inside the Corncob grid for layout unless the component docs specify another layout.
Apply content changes before visual or behavioral enhancements.
Enhance only after the canonical markup is correct and validated.
avoid: Inventing wrapper elements around required component children.
Replacing Corncob classes with a second framework or custom utility system.
Starting from a custom interpretation of a component instead of the documented example.

header.name: Header
header.description: Page header with company identity, title, navigation, and actions.
header.parentLayoutRule: Use the Corncob grid around the header for page layout. Do not add extra wrappers inside the corn-header unless the docs explicitly show them.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not wrap the required direct children in a custom brand container.
Do not replace corn-button-bar navigation with custom nav button classes when the documented example uses corn-button and corn-button--sm.
Do not move corn-header--title inside corn-company.
enhancementOrder: Start with the documented header markup.
Verify the direct child order of corn-header.
Replace placeholder content and icons.
Add page-level grid layout around the header if needed.
Add optional visual customization without breaking the documented structure.
header.example:
Logo
Company
Title
Link 1Link 2

footer.name: Footer
footer.description: Page footer with intro copy, grouped link sections, social links, and copyright.
footer.parentLayoutRule: Use the Corncob grid around the footer for page layout. Preserve the documented footer sections inside the component.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not replace corn-footer--content with an unrelated custom grid wrapper when the documented footer structure already provides grouping.
Do not move social links or copyright outside corn-footer unless the docs explicitly show a variant.
enhancementOrder: Start with the documented footer markup.
Replace placeholder headings, links, and company information.
Place the footer within page layout using the Corncob grid if needed.
Add optional visual customization after the canonical structure is correct.
footer.example:
Footer introduction.

Company

  • About Us
GitHub
© 2026 Your Company. All rights reserved.

button.name: Button
button.description: Primary action and interactive button component with multiple variants and sizes
button.accessibility: Fully keyboard accessible, proper ARIA roles, supports disabled state

text-input.name: Text Input
text-input.description: Text input field with label support and status messaging for validation feedback
text-input.accessibility: Paired with label, supports aria-invalid, includes assistive text support
text-input.parentLayoutRule: Place form controls within the Corncob grid for page layout, but preserve the corn-form--item and corn-text-input structure inside the control.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not separate the input and label into unrelated wrapper elements.
Do not omit corn-form--item when generating standard form layouts.
Do not replace the documented label association with placeholder text alone.
enhancementOrder: Start with the documented text input structure.
Ensure id and for attributes match.
Add status text or validation state.
Apply page-level grid layout around the field if needed.
Add optional visual or behavioral enhancement last.
text-input.example: status text

checkbox.name: Checkbox
checkbox.description: Checkbox input for single or grouped selections with support for disabled and indeterminate states
checkbox.accessibility: Standard HTML checkbox with label association, keyboard accessible
checkbox.parentLayoutRule: Use the Corncob grid around checkbox groups for section layout, but keep grouped checkboxes inside fieldset and legend markup.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not replace a grouped checkbox fieldset with standalone div elements.
Do not give grouped checkboxes unrelated name values when they belong to the same group.
Do not omit legend text for grouped checkbox controls.
enhancementOrder: Start with fieldset, legend, and corn-checkbox items.
Set ids and matching label for attributes.
Apply checked, disabled, inline, or indeterminate states.
Use the Corncob grid around the group if the page layout requires it.
checkbox.example: Options

radio.name: Radio Button
radio.description: Radio button for single selection within a group
radio.accessibility: Standard HTML radio button with proper grouping and label association
radio.parentLayoutRule: Use the Corncob grid around radio groups for section layout, but preserve fieldset, legend, and radio item structure inside the component.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not replace the radio group fieldset with unrelated wrapper markup.
Do not use different name values for options in the same radio group.
Do not omit legend text when grouping radios.
enhancementOrder: Start with fieldset, legend, and corn-radio-button items.
Set ids and matching label for attributes.
Apply checked, disabled, or inline states.
Use the Corncob grid around the group if the page layout requires it.
radio.example: Options

link.name: Link
link.description: Navigation links with optional styling for stand-alone usage
link.accessibility: Semantic HTML anchor tags, keyboard navigation support

link-list.name: Link List
link-list.description: A styled list of links for navigation or related content groups
link-list.accessibility: Semantic HTML list with anchor tags, keyboard navigation support
link-list.example:
• Link list item one
  • Link list item two
  • Link list item three

message.name: Message
message.description: Status banners for displaying informational, success, error, and warning messages
message.accessibility: Semantic structure with icon support and heading hierarchy
message.example:
✓
Success

Action completed successfully.

tooltip.name: Tooltip
tooltip.description: Web Component tooltip that displays on hover or focus with automatic positioning
tooltip.accessibility: Automatically manages aria-labelledby and focus, keyboard accessible

popover.name: Popover
popover.description: Web Component popover panel with flexible positioning and content support
popover.accessibility: Keyboard accessible trigger; closes on outside click and ESC key
popover.parentLayoutRule: Place popover anchors inside the Corncob grid when needed, but preserve the trigger and corn-popover relationship inside the anchor wrapper.
requiredStructure: trigger aria-controls matching the popover id
forbiddenPatterns: Do not separate the trigger and corn-popover into different containers.
Do not omit the anchor wrapper around the trigger and popover.
Do not use an aria-controls value that does not match the popover id.
enhancementOrder: Start with corn-popover--anchor, trigger, and corn-popover.
Match the trigger aria-controls to the popover id.
Add position and content.
Place the anchor in the page layout using the Corncob grid if needed.
popover.example: • Item 1

expandable.name: Expandable
expandable.description: Web Component animated expand/collapse panel built on native <details> element with smooth transitions
expandable.accessibility: Built on native <details>/<summary> for keyboard and screen-reader support

panel-menu.name: Panel Menu
panel-menu.description: Vertical navigation menu with collapsible sub-items using expandables
panel-menu.accessibility: Keyboard navigation with proper ARIA attributes
panel-menu.example: Item 1Item 2

select.name: Select
select.description: Custom dropdown with keyboard navigation (planned, not yet implemented)

toggle.name: Toggle
toggle.description: On/off switch component (planned, not yet fully implemented)

card.name: Card
card.description: Content container with optional header, body, and footer (planned, not yet implemented)

modal.name: Modal
modal.description: Accessible dialog/modal with focus trap and ESC support (planned, not yet implemented)
modal.accessibility: Use native <dialog> for built-in focus management, keyboard support, and dismiss behavior

usage.trigger: Use a button with commandfor matching the modal id and command='show-modal'
usage.closeAction: Use a button with commandfor matching the modal id and command='close'
notes: closedby='any' allows outside click and ESC key to dismiss
Keep modal title in header and content in corn-modal--content
Prefer native dialog semantics instead of custom ARIA dialog wrappers

fullExamples.minimal: Basic modal content.

tabs.name: Tabs
tabs.description: Horizontal tab navigation (planned, not yet implemented)

table.name: Table
table.description: Data table with striped rows and hover states (planned, not yet implemented)

alert.name: Alert
alert.description: Status banners (planned, not yet implemented - see Message component)

badge.name: Badge
badge.description: Small status or count indicator (planned, not yet implemented)

avatar.name: Avatar
avatar.description: User avatar with image or initials fallback (planned, not yet implemented)

button-bar.name: Button Bar
button-bar.description: Web Component toolbar with automatic overflow handling (planned, not yet implemented)

panel.name: Panel
panel.description: Content container with border and padding for grouping related content.
panel.parentLayoutRule: Use the Corncob grid to arrange panels on the page. The panel itself is the content container and should not be replaced by a custom card wrapper.
forbiddenPatterns: Do not replace corn-panel with a custom card class when Corncob panel already fits the need.
Do not rely on nested custom wrapper divs before using a panel.
enhancementOrder: Start with a single element using corn-panel.
Add semantic headings and content.
Place panels inside the Corncob grid for page layout.
Use nested panels only when the content hierarchy requires it.

recommendedSystemPrompt: You are an expert frontend developer using the Corncob Design Language. Use only `corn-*` class names and vanilla HTML/JS. Never use Tailwind, Bootstrap, or any other CSS framework. Prioritize accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), semantic HTML, and clean code. Use the official component variants and sizes exactly as documented in components.json. Use the Corncob grid for page and section layout unless a component page documents a different internal layout. Start from the canonical component code before adding enhancements or custom wrappers.
aiUsageTips: Use corn-container, corn-row, and corn-col-* for page and section layout by default.
Start from the canonical component example before adding custom styles, wrappers, or behavior.
Preserve required direct children and child order for documented components such as headers.
Always pair inputs with labels using proper for/id associations
Use semantic HTML elements where possible (button, nav, main, fieldset, legend, details, etc.)
For checkboxes and radio buttons, always use fieldset + legend when grouping
Wrap form elements in corn-form--item for consistent spacing
Use corn-form--item > corn-text-input for text inputs with labels
Use corn-status element for validation messages and status text
For expandables and popovers, ensure proper ARIA attributes and focus management
Tooltips and popovers require the anchor class wrapping trigger and content
Panel menus should use corn-expandable for nested collapsible items
Messages should include appropriate icon SVGs and semantic heading hierarchy
Test keyboard navigation for all interactive components
Use assistive text class for small helper labels
